text: Plan does not argue why it should jump queue ahead of #57, signing rollout, and multi-host sprint — just assumes priority without making the case
Latency premise is stale: think:false change cut Pi Ollama from ~90s to ~2s; async-queue justification was built on outdated assumption
OpenRouter will be systematically eliminated by the current router (per-token + semi-trusted loses to free + trusted Ollama); trust model for third-party cloud provider is undefined
Schema v2 first not justified: Hugin already records rich journal telemetry; orchestratorRating alone is not a learning loop without task-class labels and baselines
infer_direct with documentation-only guard is a security regression inconsistent with Hugin's existing scanning/provenance/signing posture
Hugin is a single serial dispatcher, not a parallel worker pool — delegation does not provide fan-out; the value proposition was misstated
No explicit success gates: the plan is a hypothesis, not a gated bet; project already uses explicit gates for equivalent-sized bets (MBA before Mac Studio)
#57 gate is accepted too narrowly — all new task submission paths (not just hugin_rate) increase completion-path churn before non-atomic completion is fixed
Response proposes priority:high / Group: for queue starvation but Group: already has semantics in the codebase; Priority: field would be new scope with parser + scheduler changes
New cloud-third-party trust tier increases coupling before reversibility is earned; removing OpenRouter later would be cross-cutting, not just deleting one executor
Revised build order is still implementation-then-validation; approved next architectural move is DIY multi-host on Munin; orchestrator stack has not displaced that with evidence
